Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Deposition
Off Calendar Jun-13-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Daniel Berko (415-771-6174) for Plaintiff/Moving party Ricky Camacho.
Jill K. Cohoe (415-400-0890) of Manning & Kass Ellrod, Ramirez, Trester LLP for Defendant/opposing party Steven Miyake.

The Court announces its Tentative Ruling. ARGUED re: production of screenshots of computer; Defendant argues they will not produce until Plaintiff pays the cost of forensic computer analyst to produce the Meta-Data; Plaintiff argues screenshots do not require forensic computer analyst. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Deposition and Production of Documents is Granted in part and denied in part. Motion is granted as to the production of documents. The screenshots indicating the date of creation of the documents at issue shall be produced within 15 days of notice of entry of the order. Motion to compel deposition is moot. Request for sanctions is denied. Order is signed in open court. (See Scanned Order for Details.) Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. (D501)

Defendant Steven Miyake'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-05-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

ATTY. JENNIFER L. SUPMAN OF MANNING & KASS, TEL. NO. 1-415-400-0801 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - Mr. Berko informed the court that the subpoena was withdrawn as to himself. There are no documents being witheld other than privilege attorney-client work product. Defendant Steven Miyake'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And For Sanctions - Further responses to be given by June 12, 2017. The request for monetary sanction is denied. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312.
